content.claim: Education Minister Judit Lannert stated that the secondary-school admissions system tied to 6/8-year gimnaziums imposes serious burdens on children and families and deserves social consultation, explicitly disavowing proposals for abolition; the opposition (Fidesz parliamentary leader Gulyás Gergely) publicly reframed this as a plan to impose a 'unified four-year secondary model' not seen since communism, linking it to regime-change reversals.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The minister's actual proposal remains limited to 'social consultation' rather than a concrete legislative text as of July 2026
The opposition's characterization as abolition-by-stealth is a political interpretation, not a documented fact about the government's intentions
No legislative proposal has been formally tabled in Parliament confirming the scope of intended reform
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
